item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was brief yet interesting. You didn't oversell the premise, and the reactions described helped flesh it out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's interesting: this is basically "radio messages from an alternate reality", which we have more than one article of on the wiki, but casting it as anomalous e-mails make it feel almost fresh. The approach to the messages as well, more than just "spooky alternate history stuff", isn't bad, either.

Novote at the moment, but I may give this another look in the future. It's intriguing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I noticed that too and immediately self-handwaved it as part of the anomaly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Minor nitpick, '+' is not a valid character in an email domain. If this was done intentionally to avoid using a real email address, fair enough.

item.title: I like it!
item.content:encoded: It’s an interesting premise and pretty well executed. The way it sounds, M&W could end up with its own Hub Page if it becomes a big enough thing, or if you decide to expand on the universes behind it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for the feedback.

+ Response to your comments
- Response to your comments

Each email is intended to be from a different universe, where Magnum and Whitley are responding to some sort of catastrophic event. In the first three emails, they're trying to carry on as usual / react to events in a practical manner. This is carried over into the recipients' behaviour after reading the email.

The last email is from a dying, desperate man who's given up all hope. There's nothing to do and nothing that can be done, except send one final email and die - and that's what drives the recipients to do what they do.

Maybe I need to be putting more emphasis on the finality of the situation in that final email.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think this is very interesting (and sitting at my workplace right now, oh my god, we take security so seriously here) and very relatable. It’s well executed, but I’m struggling as a reader to understand the last log and it’s significance. You may need to be blunter here; Scp articles do not lend themselves particularly well to subtlety.

item.title: My first SCP Article!
item.content:encoded:
This is the first SCP article I've written after 8 or so years of lurking on this site. I hope you enjoy it, and if you have any feedback on the article or my writing style, I'd love to hear it.

Thanks to Wazikamawata and AquaBlack for detailed critiques of my draft, and to those in #site19 who gave me my first round of comments a few nights ago.

Image Credits
Image Credits

Image in tab 1 -https://www.pexels.com/photo/business-business-men-businessman-confident-262380/
Image in tab 2 - https://www.flickr.com/photos/149481436@N03/35001115431
Image in tab 3 - https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Suresh_Krishna_Businessman.jpg

All other logos / graphics were created by me.
